subject: English Language
content_standard: B.6.1.1:Demonstrate understanding of variety of songs
indicator_description: Relate the central messages in songs to personal experiences
examplars:
1. Engage learners to sing some familiar songs from their cultural background.
2. Write lines of songs on chalk board and have learners read through the lines.
3. Demonstrate singing of the songs as learners listen attentively.
4. Guide learners to sing songs with stress and rhythm by clapping.
5. Invite learners to sing individually and in groups.

subject: English Language
content_standard: B.6.1.1:Demonstrate understanding of variety of songs
indicator_description: Compose songs around values discussed
examplars:
1. Have learners identify some familiar songs taught.
2. Guide them to sing identified songs with stress and rhythm.
3. Discuss the meaning of the songs and morals they teach.
4. Discuss values in the songs e.g. love, honesty etc.
5. Ensure appropriate vocabulary and expressions are used to discuss the moral values.
6. Put learners into small groups to compose songs around the values

subject: English Language
content_standard: B6.1.3.1: Appreciate poems and other pieces of literary materials
indicator_description: Relate the central messages of poems to personal experiences
examplars:
1. Engage learners in reciting some familiar poems.
2. Introduce learners to an eight-line poem on the board.
3. Read/recite the poem clapping/tapping its rhythm.
4. Have learners read lines of the poem ensuring correct pronunciation of words.
5. Let learners take turns to read in groups and individually.
6. Have learners discuss the central message and relate it to their personal experiences.

subject: English Language
content_standard: B6.1.3.1: Appreciate poems and other pieces of literary materials
indicator_description: Discuss values in poems and talk about the importance of these values in society
examplars:
1. Engage learners to recite some familiar poems.
2. Discuss the meaning of lines using appropriate expressions and vocabulary of a selected poem.
3. Guide learners with questions to identify some values in the lines recited.
4. Have them relate the values identified to day-to-day living.

subject: English Language
content_standard: B6.1.3.1: Appreciate poems and other pieces of literary materials
indicator_description: Compose eight-line poems
examplars:
1. Engage learners to recite some familiar poems.
2. Discuss the process of poem composition and lead learners to write a "class" poem.
3. Have learners work in groups to write some meaningful lines on a given topic.
4. Guide learners to put the lines or sentences together to constitute a meaningful eight-line poem. Have groups recite/perform their poems to the class.

subject: English Language
content_standard: B6.1.4.1: Respond to stories
indicator_description: Make connections between texts or stories and personal experiences.
examplars:
1. Have the learners recall one or two stories read/heard.
2. Tell or read a story to learners.
3. Invite individuals to retell the story sequentially using story map/herringbone strategy.
4. Lead the class to discuss the story and guide them to relate the story to their personal experiences.

subject: English Language
content_standard: B6.1.4.1: Respond to stories
indicator_description: Tell stories around given themes
examplars:
1. Discuss themes in story telling.
2. Tell a story to learners
3. Guide learners with questions to identify the theme of the story e.g. love, honesty, patriotism, hard work, etc.
4. Based on the example, have learners tell stories around given themes.
5. With examples encourage the use of appropriate vocabulary to depict chosen themes.

subject: English Language
content_standard: B6.1.5.1 Appreciate pieces of literary materials through dramatisation
indicator_description: Dramatise/role-play whole/parts of stories/scenes, events
examplars:
1. Guide learners through discussion to identify some stories/scenes, events etc.
2. Discuss the plot of the chosen story i.e. events at the beginning, middle, and ending.
3. Have learners prepare and dramatise/role-play whole/parts of stories/scene, events).
4. Ensure the role-play/dramatisation depicts selected theme.
5. Ensure appropriate vocabulary use as well as correct pronunciation.

subject: English Language
content_standard: B6.1.5.2: Appreciate key issues in stories/sketches
indicator_description: Express personal opinion about moral lessons in sketches
examplars:
1. Guide learners with questions to identify the structure (beginning, middle and ending) of familiar stories, e.g. Maame Akua at the Market.
2. Lead learners to discuss the plot of the particular story chosen.
3. Guide learners to role-play the story as others listen and observe and relate them to real life situations.
4. Using an example from the story guide learners to identify some moral values.
5. Learners express personal opinions about the moral lessons identified.

subject: English Language
content_standard: B6.1.5.2: Appreciate key issues in stories/sketches
indicator_description: Write plays out of a story and perform them
examplars:
1. Learners read out a story from textbooks or readers.
2. Through discussion, guide learners to tell the plot of the story.
3. Show a sample, and guide learners to write a play with the story told.
4. Have learners produce the written play around the identified themes and values.

subject: English Language
content_standard: B6.1.5.2: Appreciate key issues in stories/sketches
indicator_description: Justify the central messages in sketches performed
examplars:
1. Have learner discuss a sketch observed.
2. Learners say what to look for to identify key issues in a sketch.
3. Learners talk about lessons in the sketch.

subject: English Language
sub_strand: Conversation - Talking about Oneself, Family, People, Customs, Socials/Cultural Values and Manners
content_standard: B6.1.6.1: Explore certain culturally acceptable language for communication
indicator_description: Describe/talk about objects/personalities/ events in the country and the world
examplars:
1. Revise previous discussions on the following: i. Objects - objects found in class, at home etc. ii. Dates - dates of birth. iii. Time - various times at school in the day e.g. break time. iv. Names of regions - Central, Ashanti, Northern, etc.
2. Lead a discussion on: i. Personalities - e.g. role models, MPs, head of institution ii. Event - festivals, games, special occasions e.g. Christmas
3. Guide learners to use appropriate vocabulary in the discussion.
4. Encourage learners to ask and answer questions for clarification of other learners' opinions.

subject: English Language
sub_strand: Conversation - Talking about Oneself, Family, People, Customs, Socials/Cultural Values and Manners
content_standard: B6.1.6.2:Demonstrate positive listening and viewing attitudes and behaviour by showing attentiveness and understanding
indicator_description: View and listen attentively and for a sustained period, (e.g., look at the person speaking and maintain eye contact) and record the key issues in the discussion
examplars:
1. Introduce and explain the activity.
2. Demonstrate the activity/show or play it on the tape /video
3. Have groups/pairs converse on given topics as others observe and critique/ask questions for clarification
4. Encourage participants in a conversational to listen attentively and maintain eye contact.

subject: English Language
sub_strand: Conversation - Talking about Oneself, Family, People, Customs, Socials/Cultural Values and Manners
content_standard: B6.1.6.2:Demonstrate positive listening and viewing attitudes and behaviour by showing attentiveness and understanding
indicator_description: Listen and view for the entire duration of text/speech/presentation/video etc.
examplars:
1. Together with learners choose a text from their readers which has already been read.
2. Discuss the text e.g. title, characters, simple literary terms.
3. Guide learners to discuss the character traits of some characters and role-play them.
4. Pause to ask learners questions in the process, e. g. Why do you think the characters said that?

subject: English Language
sub_strand: Conversation - Talking about Oneself, Family, People, Customs, Socials/Cultural Values and Manners
content_standard: B6.1.6.3: Use knowledge of language and communicative skills to participate in conversation
indicator_description: Engage in collaborative conversation with unfamiliar audience
examplars:
1. Show a video of a talk show or introduce a talk show by drawing on learners' background knowledge of such activities on TV.
2. Identify an interesting topic e.g. "Children should not do WhatsApp". Initiate a conversation using questions. Learners take turns to contribute to the conversation.
3. Put learners in groups and let them choose topics for conversation. Go round to ensure learners take turns, use facial expressions appropriately and maintain eye contact when in conversation.

subject: English Language
sub_strand: Conversation - Talking about Oneself, Family, People, Customs, Socials/Cultural Values and Manners
content_standard: B6.1.6.3: Use knowledge of language and communicative skills to participate in conversation
indicator_description: Demonstrate turn taking in conversation on different topics and follow agreed-upon rules for conversation and express thoughts coherently
examplars:
1. Through discussion, guide learners to identify some current or recent events.
2. Choose one such event and engage in a model conversation with a learner earlier prepared.
3. Converse on a given topic with a learner as others watch.
4. Let learners, converse in pairs on different topics after the example. Encourage them to follow the rules of conversation.
5. Guide the use of appropriate vocabulary by showing vocabulary cards and indirectly dropping hints of a correct word.

subject: English Language
sub_strand: Conversation - Talking about Oneself, Family, People, Customs, Socials/Cultural Values and Manners
content_standard: B6.1.6.3: Use knowledge of language and communicative skills to participate in conversation
indicator_description: Ask and answer questions about key details on topics under discussion.
examplars:
1. Put learners into groups to discuss topics such as "How I spend my holidays; My future career" etc.
2. Encourage learners to ask and answer questions for clarification about key details.
3. Ensure appropriate vocabulary use by dropping hints and showing word cards.

subject: English Language
content_standard: B6.1.7.1: Use appropriate skills and strategies to process meaning from texts
indicator_description: Employ Think Aloud and visualisation strategies to make meaning from level-appropriate texts
examplars:
1. Have learners listen to teacher-read texts or video/audio recording on familiar topics.
2. Model the visualisation strategy to make meaning from texts heard.
3. Encourage learners to practise constructing meaning from the texts or play.

subject: English Language
content_standard: B6.1.7.1: Use appropriate skills and strategies to process meaning from texts
indicator_description: Relate the sequence of events (E.g. beginning, middle and end)stories/drama/texts
examplars:
1. Referring to a recently read story, assist learners to identify the sequence of events in the story. e.g. The beginning, middle, and ending.
2. Groups choose a story read or heard and identify the plot structure and list events at the beginning, middle and at the end.

subject: English Language
content_standard: B6.1.7.1: Use appropriate skills and strategies to process meaning from texts
indicator_description: Ask relevant questions to improve on understanding of moral lesson in text.
examplars:
1. Revise the sequence of events in a familiar story/drama/text with learners.
2. Together with learners, identify the characters/the settings and moral lessons in the story/drama/text read.
3. Discuss the roles of characters, setting and the moral values in the text.
4. Ask relevant questions to improve understanding of elements in the texts.
5. Encourage learners to also ask question.

subject: English Language
content_standard: B6.1.7.1: Use appropriate skills and strategies to process meaning from texts
indicator_description: Use background knowledge to understand and build new knowledge while listening to informational text
examplars:
1. Present an informational text on a given topic.
2. Use the K and W of KWL strategy to activate learners' background knowledge.
3. Have learners listen to the text.
4. In groups, they identify and discuss the main ideas of the text.
5. Have learners listen to the text a second time.
6. In groups, learners share what they have learnt in the text.

subject: English Language
content_standard: B6.1.7.1: Use appropriate skills and strategies to process meaning from texts
indicator_description: Express own opinions about the details of texts
examplars:
1. In pairs/groups, learners share the knowledge acquired from details of a story/drama/text heard.
2. Put learners into groups to express personal opinions about details of texts.

subject: English Language
content_standard: B6.1.7.1: Use appropriate skills and strategies to process meaning from texts
indicator_description: Identify the problems and solutions in texts
examplars:
1. Ask questions for learners to recall the events and values in stories read.
2. Guide them to analyse these into cause(s) effect(s) and solution(s).

subject: English Language
content_standard: B6.1.7.1: Use appropriate skills and strategies to process meaning from texts
indicator_description: Make simple generalisations based on the main ideas and key details of a text
examplars:
1. Have learners listen to a story and identify the main ideas and key details.
2. Use questions to guide learners to make generalizations based on the main ideas and key details.

subject: English Language
content_standard: B6.1.8.1: Demonstrate understanding in asking and answering questions correctly
indicator_description: Answer “Yes” or “No” questions correctly using the expressions that show the future
examplars:
1. Through discussion, let learners identify an activity they will perform at a future time, e.g. tomorrow, next month etc.
2. Ask questions to elicit “Yes” or “No” answers e.g. Will you come to school tomorrow? Yes/No; Yes, I will/No, I won’t.
3. Introduce drills e.g. Questions and Answers Drill, for learners to practise asking and answering “Yes” or “No” Questions.

subject: English Language
content_standard: B6.1.8.2: Identify and use question tags correctly in speech
indicator_description: Use positive tags, negative tags and auxiliaries in speech
examplars:
1. Put learners in pairs to ask and answer simple questions in turns.
2. Discuss positive tags (e.g. It is cold, isn’t it?) and negative tags e.g. It isn’t cold, is it?
3. Demonstrate with learners the usage of positive tags, negative tags and auxiliaries in speech.
4. Pair learners to take turns to practise positive tags, negative tags and auxiliaries in speech.

subject: English Language
sub_strand: Giving and Following Commands/ Instructions/Directions and Making and Responding to Requests
content_standard: B6.1.9.1: Explore the use of verbs in commands, instructions, requests and directions appropriately in speech.
indicator_description: Create and present simple instructions or a manual on how to play a game
examplars:
1. Give sample instructions on how to play a game.
2. Put learners into convenient groups to study and discuss the instructions.
3. Have each group choose a game and create instructions on how to play it.
4. Have learners present their work to the class for feedback.
5. Learners role-play.

subject: English Language
sub_strand: Giving and Following Commands/ Instructions/Directions and Making and Responding to Requests
content_standard: B6.1.9.1: Explore the use of verbs in commands, instructions, requests and directions appropriately in speech.
indicator_description: Make and respond to polite requests for help from unfamiliar people
examplars:
1. Discuss requests e.g. as an act of asking politely or formally for something.
2. Demonstrate making polite requests with two or three learners.
3. Let learners role-play making and responding to polite requests.
4. Guide learners with given situations to role-play making and responding to polite requests for help from unfamiliar people.

subject: English Language
content_standard: B6.1.10.1: Demonstrate knowledge of spoken grammar and register
indicator_description: Demonstrate awareness of the features of spoken language e.g. the use of modal expressions to convey tentativeness e.g. probably, not too sure etc.
examplars:
1. Revise important features of spoken language such as contractions and ellipsis (contractions, ellipsis e.g. A: What’s your name? B: Esi.
2. Let learners identify these features in their readers, story books, etc.
3. Let them engage in pairs and group dialogue on given topics.

subject: English Language
content_standard: B6.1.10.1: Demonstrate knowledge of spoken grammar and register
indicator_description: Demonstrate awareness of the differences between spoken and written forms of language (simplicity/complexity of sentence structures, formal/informal)
examplars:
1. Lead learners to discover differences between spoken and written forms of language. E.g. Formal and informal language: "I can't " vs "I cannot".
2. Create situations for learners to practise both formal and informal forms.

subject: English Language
content_standard: B6.1.10.1: Demonstrate knowledge of spoken grammar and register
indicator_description: Demonstrate awareness of how meaning is conveyed through appropriate pace, stress, tone – through news reporting
examplars:
1. Revise how meaning is conveyed through appropriate pace, stress and tone.
2. Model presenting news or have learners watch a news presentation.
3. Put learners into groups to discuss pace, stress and tone of news presented.
4. Guide learners to prepare and present news on given topics.

subject: English Language
content_standard: B6.1.10.2: Demonstrate the ability to communicate with accurate pronunciation and appropriate intonation
indicator_description: Speak at a good pace and with expression
examplars:
1. Demonstrate a model speech: Choose a familiar topic and make a short but moving speech on it.
2. Have learners discuss the speech focusing on its pace and expressiveness.
3. Let learners speak on very short and simple topics using appropriate voice quality.
4. The class comment on the relevant features.

subject: English Language
content_standard: B6.1.10.2: Demonstrate the ability to communicate with accurate pronunciation and appropriate intonation
indicator_description: Read aloud clearly, at a good pace and with expression
examplars:
1. Give a model reading by reading a short texts aloud.
2. Have pupils read aloud in pairs and in small groups.
3. Let them write and read out short speeches on given topics in groups.
4. Groups may read out their speeches to the class.

subject: English Language
content_standard: B6.1.10.3: Plan and present information and ideas for a variety of purposes
indicator_description: Research and deliver speeches on given informational topics
examplars:
1. Have learners select topics of interest from informational texts read.
2. Help learners to identify the purpose and audience of sample speeches.
3. Have learners visit the library to research and plan their speeches.

subject: English Language
content_standard: B6.1.10.3: Plan and present information and ideas for a variety of purposes
indicator_description: Draw on prior knowledge to identify subject matter and organisational structure of speech
examplars:
1. Help learners to draw on their prior knowledge about speech making. For instance, ensure that learners know the roles of participants, facilitators, advisors and time-keepers in the group.
2. Learners must be conversant with subject matter and the (organisational) structure of their speech.
3. Guide learners to choose their own topics for a speech.

subject: English Language
content_standard: B6.1.10.3: Plan and present information and ideas for a variety of purposes
indicator_description: Gather and select facts and ideas from one or multiple print and/ or non-print sources, appropriate to the purpose, audience, context and culture
examplars:
1. Through discussion, let learners generate ideas and details appropriate to the purpose, audience, context and culture on given topics.
2. Provide model speeches (e.g. downloaded from the internet) for discussion. These will help learners to generate ideas for their speeches.
3. Guide the learners to download important speeches from the internet. Discuss the organisational structure of the speeches with them.

subject: English Language
content_standard: B6.1.10.3: Plan and present information and ideas for a variety of purposes
indicator_description: Support ideas and points of view with concrete examples to convey meaning appropriate to purpose and context.
examplars:
1. Provide sample speech topics
2. Through discussion, guide learners to select facts and ideas from one or more sources such as print sources and non print sources appropriate to the purpose, audience context and culture.

subject: English Language
content_standard: B6.1.10.3: Plan and present information and ideas for a variety of purposes
indicator_description: Identify and use the appropriate register for formal and informal contexts
examplars:
1. Present sample speeches to groups to study.
2. Provide topics and guide learners to identify related key vocabulary.

subject: English Language
content_standard: B6.1.10.3: Plan and present information and ideas for a variety of purposes
indicator_description: Elaborate on points using experiences and feelings
examplars:
1. Provide topics and lead class discussions to: - generate ideas; - expand the points with such details such as facts, examples, explanations and personal experiences.
2. Learners repeat the activity in pairs and in groups.

subject: English Language
content_standard: B6.1.10.3: Plan and present information and ideas for a variety of purposes
indicator_description: Speak with confidence before different audiences using appropriate verbal and non-verbal cues to convey meaning
examplars:
1. Through discussion, explain the need to be able to speak with confidence before an audience.
2. Show video clips of well-known persons delivering speeches for learners to observe and comment on.
3. Let learners practise these in pairs and groups.

subject: English Language
content_standard: B6.2.2.1: Connect sounds to letters; and blend letters/syllables in order to read and write
indicator_description: Read words with ending sounds like "sure" as in measure; "ture" as in creature and "tch" as in Stretch
examplars:
1. Use word games to introduce words with ending sounds like "sure" as in measure; "ture" as in creature and "tch" as in Stretch.
2. Guide learners to play the Pick and Read game to practise reading the target words.
3. Have groups write sentences with the target words and read out the sentences to the class.

subject: English Language
content_standard: B6.2.2.1: Connect sounds to letters; and blend letters/syllables in order to read and write
indicator_description: Read ccvcc, cccvc words and multisyllabic words when reading continuous texts.
examplars:
1. Provide texts having multisyllabic words for regular practise by learners.
2. Model the production of the ccvcc and the cccvc words.
3. Ask learners to repeat the ccvcc and the cccvc words after you while you check in correct discrepancies in the pronunciation of the words. e.g. : ccvcc words - trust, striped etc. cccvc words - scrap, sprite etc. Multisyllabic words - communication, immediate etc.

subject: English Language
sub_strand: Word Families, minimal pairs and Common Digraphs
content_standard: B6.2.3.1: Identify minimal pairs and common digraphs
indicator_description: Use common minimal pairs to decode words
examplars:
1. Give examples of minimal pairs
2. Using “think-pair-share”, let learners come up with more examples of minimal pairs. E.g. Two sounds difference (goat, coat, boat, road, loan), One sound difference (wall, ball, train, chain, Bridge, fridge)
3. Introduce and revise these and other similar spelling patterns using examples (two or three patterns at a time).
4. Drill the pronunciation of the sounds.
5. Have learners read several words having the spelling pattern.
6. Let learners also read the words in context.

subject: English Language
sub_strand: Word Families, minimal pairs and Common Digraphs
content_standard: B6.2.3.1: Identify rhyming/endings words and common digraphs
indicator_description: Use words with digraphs to make meaningful sentences
examplars: 1. Have learners identify words having specific digraphs and form meaningful sentences with the words. e.gs. of digraphs: ch-chair ck-duck ph- phone ng- sting sh- ship qu- queen wh-whale

subject: English Language
content_standard: B6.2.4.1: Identify and use diphthongs to decode texts
indicator_description: Use words with centering diphthongs ( e.g. / iǝ, eǝ, Ʊǝ ) to make meaningful sentences
examplars:
1. In a discussion have learners talk about diphthongs.
2. Give several examples and elicit examples from them. /iǝ/ - fear, ear, near, clear; /eǝ/ - prepare, there, stairs; /Ʊǝ/ - pure, secure
3. Have learners use words with diphthongs in meaningful sentences.

subject: English Language
content_standard: B6.2.5.1: Identify and use consonant blends and clusters in reading
indicator_description: Orally produce three-syllable words by blending sounds (phonemes), including consonant blends
examplars:
1. Revise consonant blends and clusters using several spelling patterns e.g. bl, br, cl, cr, gl, gr, spr, scr, str, chr.
2. Drill the pronunciation of the consonant blends and clusters in isolation and in words.
3. Learners work in groups to identify and list words having the blends and clusters.
4. Have learners read the words in isolation and in context.

subject: English Language
content_standard: B6.2.5.1: Identify and use consonant blends and clusters in reading
indicator_description: Use the spelling-sound correspondences for common consonant digraphs
examplars:
1. In a discussion, talk about digraphs as a whole class, group and in pairs.
2. Have learners try to establish some rules in forming and spelling words having digraphs. E.g. ch as in church, sh as in shirt, gh as in tough

subject: English Language
content_standard: B6.2.6.1: Understand word meanings and usages
indicator_description: Use level-appropriate content words (nouns, verbs, adjectives and adverbs) and function words (prepositions) appropriately in spoken and written communication
examplars:
1. Using examples, explain simply the two groups of words.
2. Elicit sentences and have learners identify the two categories of words from their sentences.
3. Have sentences on sentence strips on the board or cardboards and have learners identify the function words and content words.
4. Have learners make meaningful sentences on their own and share with peers or in groups.

subject: English Language
content_standard: B6.2.6.1: Understand word meanings and usages
indicator_description: Use the following terms: compound words, idiom, simile, synonym, antonym, pre-fix, suffix, phrasal verb etc. in spoken and written expressions
examplars:
1. Introduce the terms one at a time using several examples.
2. Let learners identify the terms in the texts and stories they read.
3. Have learners use idioms, similes, compound words, synonyms, antonyms etc. in meaningful sentences.

subject: English Language
content_standard: B6.2.6.2:Build vocabulary
indicator_description: Develop a rich vocabulary stock through extensive reading of age-appropriate text; substituting selected nouns verbs and adjectives in a text with synonyms /near synonyms.
examplars: 1. Have learners regularly play several vocabulary games such as “Word hunt”, “Lucky Dip”, “I know it”, etc. Let them build a portfolio on vocabulary and use them in meaningful sentences.

subject: English Language
content_standard: B6.2.6.3: Demonstrate a rich vocabulary that supports the development of listening, reading, speaking, writing and presentation skills
indicator_description: Deduce meaning of words from how they relate to one another
examplars:
1. Explain and demonstrate that the meaning of a word can be deduced from other words in its environment. E.g. Philip is glad. He is really happy. Aku is tall but her brother is short.
2. Put learners in groups. Let them read a text and l give the contextual meaning of selected words from the text.
3. Engage learners in activities such as: - Matching words with their meanings/synonyms/antonyms. - Reading a text and finding replacements for certain words in the text.
4. Using other strategies such as the synonym or antonym tree or synonym or antonym ‘bingo’, have learners build a portfolio of antonyms and synonyms.
5. Meaning from word relationships hyponyms: fruit – apple meronyms: hand – finger
6. Have learners use the vocabulary tree and mother to child to build words using hyponyms and meronyms.

subject: English Language
content_standard: B6.2.6.4: Use words appropriately for purpose, audience, context and culture
indicator_description: Recognise how words are formed through compounding
examplars:
1. Have learners refresh their knowledge on affixes – prefixes and suffixes – , acronyms and blending using examples.
2. Learners choose words with given affixes and use them appropriately in sentences.
3. Introduce compound words in context and have learners identify their components. E.g. breakfast = break, fast; classroom = class, room.
4. Put learners into groups to build more compound words.

subject: English Language
content_standard: B6.2.6.4: Use words appropriately for purpose, audience, context and culture
indicator_description: Recognise the playful use of words in spoken and written language (jokes, riddles, puns)
examplars:
1. Introduce these one at a time.
2. Provide and discuss examples.
3. Learners play games with the activity in pairs/groups.

subject: English Language
content_standard: B6.2.6.4: Use words appropriately for purpose, audience, context and culture
indicator_description: Use words suitable for purpose, audience, context and culture in relation to: type of texts (exposition/explanation, persuasive language, argumentative), medium (spoken/written), register (formal/informal) etc.
examplars:
1. Choose appropriate texts to guide learners identify the key elements in writing. E.g. purpose, audience, setting, characters, etc.
2. Have them role play a simple story to bring out the importance of these elements and their usage.

subject: English Language
content_standard: B6.2.7.1: Process and comprehend level appropriate texts
indicator_description: Construct meaning from texts
examplars:
1. Select level-appropriate texts for learners.
2. Based on background knowledge and other factors, have learners predict what a text will be about and actively adjust comprehension while reading/viewing or listening.
3. Learners connect their background knowledge to help them make meaning of the text as they read.
4. Assist learners with a variety of questions to make meaning during and after reading the text.
5. Lay emphasis on the need to use the environment of a word to get its meaning.

subject: English Language
content_standard: B6.2.7.1: Process and comprehend level appropriate texts
indicator_description: Note and recall main ideas in sequence
examplars:
1. Guide learners to present facts and ideas in a sequential order.
2. You could also adopt other strategies like the SQ3R.
3. SQ3R strategy Have learners: S – Survey the text – (learners skim by going through the chapter, note heading/sub-heading and other features. Q – Question – generate questions about the content of the text. 3R Read – read for more information. Recite – retrieve information from text. Review – go over the main ideas in the text to consolidate understanding

subject: English Language
content_standard: B6.2.7.1: Process and comprehend level appropriate texts
indicator_description: Scan/skim for details
examplars: 1. Use texts to guide learners to grasp the main ideas as they skim/scan in 3-4 minutes and have them present their points for class discussion. Note: This must be a regular feature of reading and comprehension lessons.

subject: English Language
content_standard: B6.2.7.1: Process and comprehend level appropriate texts
indicator_description: Read level-appropriate texts silently and closely for comprehension
examplars: 1. Give frequent and regular practice in silent and close reading using the Directed Reading Activity (DRA) strategy.

subject: English Language
content_standard: B6.2.7.2: Apply critical reading, implied meaning, higher order thinking, judgment and evaluation
indicator_description: Respond to a text with reason, simple judgment and personal interpretations
examplars:
1. Have learners read a short text.
2. Learners create a mental image from the text read visualising/ bringing the text to life by engaging the imagination and using all the senses e.g. (This visualisation will help bring life to the text for learners to interpret and give their opinion and personal interpretation to the write up).
3. Lead a discussion of each text read with questions. Questions should elicit : - Factual information - Implied meaning - Judgment - Evaluation - Personal Response

subject: English Language
content_standard: B6.2.7.2: Apply critical reading, implied meaning, higher order thinking, judgment and evaluation
indicator_description: Compare and contrast two or more events in a text
examplars:
1. Put learners into groups to identify, compare and contrast two or more events within a text or in different texts read or heard.
2. They may also compare and contrast other elements like characters and setting.

subject: English Language
content_standard: B6.2.7.2: Apply critical reading, implied meaning, higher order thinking, judgment and evaluation
indicator_description: Demonstrate awareness of the structure of texts (e.g. introduction, body, conclusion)
examplars:
1. Guide learners systematically using given narrative texts to identify the introduction body and conclusion in it.
2. Have learners summarise each of these parts and make a summary of the whole text using their part summaries.

subject: English Language
content_standard: B6.2.7.3: Demonstrate understanding of the use of words and phrases as used in the text
indicator_description: Determine the contextual meaning of words and phrases.
examplars:
1. Select a text for reading. Identify words and phrases and systematically, guide learners to work out their meanings in context.
2. Put learners into groups and give each group a set of words and phrases to find their meanings. Each group presents its work.

subject: English Language
content_standard: B6.2.8.1: construct meaning from text read
indicator_description: Read silently and reasonably for meaning from a level-appropriate text
examplars:
1. Have learners stop and think about the text and know what to do when meaning is not clear. By setting a target in the text for them.
2. Have them read silently and at a reasonable pace to get the meaning from the text.
3. Direct learners’ reading with “While Reading” questions.

subject: English Language
content_standard: B6.2.8.1: construct meaning from text read
indicator_description: Find meaning of words as used in context
examplars: 1. Have learners play vocabulary games that involve meaning and usage of words. E.g. Lucky Dip, Fishing.

subject: English Language
content_standard: B6.2.8.1: construct meaning from text read
indicator_description: Ask and answer questions based on a passage read
examplars:
1. Prepare learners adequately before reading activities. E.g. Discussion of background knowledge, title and accompanying pictures of the reading text, prediction.
2. Provide “While-reading” questions.
3. Have learners read the text silently.
4. Assign learners to answer a variety of questions based on the text e.g. factual, inferential and applicative questions.

subject: English Language
content_standard: B6.2.9.1: Read fluently to enhance comprehension
indicator_description: Read grade-level text with meaning
examplars:
1. Use the three-stage approach (Before Reading, While Reading, and After Reading) for learners to prepare adequately and read texts and provide adequate follow-up activities.
2. Use who, what, where, when, why and how of an event organiser to guide learners to adequately discuss the text and respond to questions on it.

subject: English Language
content_standard: B6.2.9.1: Read fluently to enhance comprehension
indicator_description: read aloud with expressions that reflect the author’s purpose and meaning.
examplars:
1. Have learners read texts independently paying attention to punctuation marks, words, sentences and paragraphs.
2. Learners use self-correction, word recognition and re-reading strategies to confirm comprehension.

subject: English Language
content_standard: B6.2.10.1: Read and summarize passages read
indicator_description: Summarise level-appropriate passages/text orally
examplars:
1. Learners identify the most important ideas and restate them in their own words.
2. Begin from sentences and short paragraphs. E.g. Sentence: This is the boy who killed the snake. Summary: The boy killed the snake.
3. Lead learners with questions to do this. E.g. What did you learn from this text?

subject: English Language
content_standard: B6.2.10.1: Read and summarize passages read
indicator_description: Write short summary of a level appropriate passage/text read
examplars:
1. Have learners read the passage.
2. Discuss it briefly with learners.
3. Working in groups, learners write a summary of the passage taking note of the main idea(s).
4. Let learners present their write up for discussion. E.g. Select main ideas from the passage without examples/illustrations/words in parenthesis.
5. Learners use the sequence order to write down the events that took place in the correct order.

subject: English Language
strand: Grammar Usage at Word and Phrase Levels
content_standard: B6.3.1.1: Apply knowledge of different types of nouns in communication
indicator_description: Identify and use nouns or noun phrases to describe conditions.

subject: English Language
strand: Grammar Usage at Word and Phrase Levels
content_standard: B6.3.1.1: Apply knowledge of different types of nouns in communication
indicator_description: Identify and use: proper nouns to refer to organisations/events, Count/non-count, Singular, Plural (regular, irregular), without plural marker, Gender
examplars:
1. Revise all the types of nouns (refer to B1- B5).
2. Provide learners with opportunities to further practise using these nouns. E.g. a. Learners take their reading books and identify and make a list of these nouns, use them in sentences and present their work to the class for feedback. b. Use a language drill to help learners do practice activities. E.g. Pair Drill for singular/plural nouns - Learner “A” gives a noun – mango - Learner “B” gives its plural form “mangoes” and uses it in a sentence – I love mangoes.
3. C – Learners play the “Lucky Dip game” for practice. Learners take turns to dip their hands into a box containing words of collective nouns, pick a card and use the word in a sentence. E.g. a team of horses, a bevy of ladies, a flock of sheep
4. D – Learners can look for types of nouns using the internet and use them in sentences.
5. Provide further practice activities to build on learners' knowledge on nouns.

subject: English Language
strand: Grammar Usage at Word and Phrase Levels
content_standard: B6.3.1.1: Apply knowledge of different types of nouns in communication
indicator_description: Use "- ing" nouns and noun phrases to refer to activities
examplars: 1. E.g. I enjoy singing. I like swimming.

subject: English Language
strand: Grammar Usage at Word and Phrase Levels
content_standard: B6.3.1.1: Apply knowledge of different types of nouns in communication
indicator_description: Identify and use abstract nouns to refer to concepts and ideas
examplars:
1. Revise abstract nouns with learners
2. Have learners identify abstract nouns in texts and these nouns in sentences e.g. intelligence, knowledge, idea, patience i. I admire Ama because of her patience. He appears to have a good knowledge.

subject: English Language
strand: Grammar Usage at Word and Phrase Levels
content_standard: B6.3.2.1: Apply knowledge of different types of determiners in communication
indicator_description: Identify and use definite and indefinite articles
examplars:
1. Briefly revise nouns. Learners read/listen to a passage having several nouns.
2. Have them identify the nouns stating their types.
3. Learners identify the nouns noting words that precede them e. g. the, an
4. Select sentences containing nouns and articles and use them to explain the use of the articles “a”, “an” and “the” simply.
5. In groups, learners write sentences and underline the articles.

subject: English Language
strand: Grammar Usage at Word and Phrase Levels
content_standard: B6.3.2.1: Apply knowledge of different types of determiners in communication
indicator_description: Identify and use quantifiers.
examplars:
1. Revise nouns and articles briefly.
2. Introduce quantifiers in context. Learners listen to or read a text having several quantifiers.
3. Learners identify the nouns and the words that go with them (quantifiers).
4. Elicit examples from learners.
5. In pairs, one says a sentence containing a qualifier/quantifier and the other identifies the quantifiers. Role(s) are reversed.

subject: English Language
strand: Grammar Usage at Word and Phrase Levels
content_standard: B6.3.2.1: Apply knowledge of different types of determiners in communication
indicator_description: Identify and use possessive pronouns to show possession
examplars:
1. Revise personal pronouns by having learners identify them in sentences and use them in sentences of their own.
2. Introduce possessive pronouns in context. E.g. i. I gave my book to Mary. ii. She sold her phone.
3. Learners listen to or read a passage containing several possessives (my, your, his, hers, its).
4. Ask them to identify words that show or indicate possession.
5. List words on the board and elicit others.
6. Have learners play a game e.g. the “Fishing Game” with cards bearing possessive pronouns. They read the word on the card and use it in a sentence.

subject: English Language
strand: Grammar Usage at Word and Phrase Levels
content_standard: B6.3.2.1: Apply knowledge of different types of determiners in communication
indicator_description: Identify and use demonstratives: this/that, these/those – concepts and ideas.
examplars:
1. Briefly revise nouns and articles by having learners identify them in sentences.
2. Provide a text having the demonstratives with emphasis on the demonstratives (this, that, these, those). Read it out.
3. List the demonstratives and have learners read out and observe the use of the demonstratives in the sentences.
4. Working in small groups, each group writes a number of sentences with each of the demonstratives introduced.

subject: English Language
strand: Grammar Usage at Word and Phrase Levels
content_standard: B6.3.3.1: Apply knowledge of different types of pronouns in communication
indicator_description: Identify and use: Reflexive pronouns, Relative pronouns, Reciprocal pronouns
examplars:
1. Use a text to revise the different pronouns learnt in B1-B5.
2. Introduce reflexive pronouns with examples in sentences.
3. Discuss the reflexive pronouns with learners.
4. Provide a passage and group learners to identify reflexive pronouns, and use the pronouns identified in sentences.
5. Repeat the procedure to teach relative and reciprocal pronouns.

subject: English Language
strand: Grammar Usage at Word and Phrase Levels
content_standard: B6.3.4.1: Apply the knowledge of adjectives in communication
indicator_description: Use comparatives forms of regular and irregular adjectives to make comparisons
examplars:
1. Revise the comparatives with learners.
2. Use practical activities to guide learners to change the positive forms of regular adjectives into comparative forms by adding -er. E.g. Compare the heights of two learners: i. Musah is tall. Safianu is short. ii. Musah is taller than Safianu.
3. Guide learners to form the superlative forms of regular adjectives by adding -est. E.g. Ali is the tallest.
4. Provide a text containing irregular forms of adjectives. Start with those that have different spelling for comparative and superlative. e.g. good better best
5. Provide a text for learners to identify the examples. Learners use the irregular forms of adjectives in sentences
6. Assist learners to use the adjectival forms position, more – and most – much more most intelligent more intelligent most intelligent
7. For each type or form, guide learners with examples to compare classroom objects and things within the vicinity of the school and beyond.

subject: English Language
strand: Grammar Usage at Word and Phrase Levels
content_standard: B6.3.5.1: Apply the knowledge of verbs in communication
indicator_description: Use different types of verbs: Main verb and Helping verb (primary auxiliary and modal auxiliary)
examplars:
1. Revise verbs generally. Have learners identify verbs in sentences and use them in their own sentences.
2. Helping verb (primary auxiliary and modal auxiliary E.g. can might, would, ought, is, are, is, are, was, were).
3. Choose a text (story) and have the learners work in groups to identify the main verbs. Let each group select five of the verbs and use them in sentences.
4. Introduce auxiliaries (primary and modal) in context one at a time. Learners listen to/read a text having several of them.
5. Guide learners with examples to identify and distinguish them from the main verbs and use them in sentences.

subject: English Language
strand: Grammar Usage at Word and Phrase Levels
content_standard: B6.3.5.1: Apply the knowledge of verbs in communication
indicator_description: Use appropriate subject-verb agreement (indefinite pronouns, Singular/plural and Collective nouns
examplars:
1. Revise subject-verb agreement concept learners have learnt in B4 and B5 by writing examples of sentences on the board and discussing them with learners.
2. Have groups of learners discuss and exemplify the following: - A singular subject goes with a singular verb. - A plural subject goes with a plural verb. - A collective subject goes with singular or plural verb.
3. Present a text. Put the class into groups to identify sentences showing subject –verb agreement involving Indefinite pronouns as subjects. Each group presents its work.
4. Conduct suitable drills for learners to have practice. Learners sit in groups to converse on a topic. E.g. “What the people in my family do daily”.

subject: English Language
strand: Grammar Usage at Word and Phrase Levels
content_standard: B6.3.5.1: Apply the knowledge of verbs in communication
indicator_description: Use different forms of verbs: Irregular, Infinitive
examplars: 1. E.g.: I asked my friend to read the story.

subject: English Language
strand: Grammar Usage at Word and Phrase Levels
content_standard: B6.3.5.1: Apply the knowledge of verbs in communication
indicator_description: Use the simple present form of verbs in sentences: For scheduled future actions, for future possibilities or plans
examplars:
1. Revise the simple present in context.
2. Present an on-going situation to learners for them to report on it. E.g. A football game/match.
3. Present similar situations for learners to describe using the simple present, in groups.
4. Groups may write and present their work to the class.
5. Use texts/sentences to introduce the use of the simple present for: - scheduled future actions E.g. The bus leaves early tomorrow. - future possibilities or plans e.g. If he doesn't come early, we shall go without him.

subject: English Language
strand: Grammar Usage at Word and Phrase Levels
content_standard: B6.3.5.1: Apply the knowledge of verbs in communication
indicator_description: Use the past continuous form of verbs to talk about actions/events which were going on when a second one took place
examplars: 1. e.g. We were playing football when the fire broke out.

subject: English Language
strand: Grammar Usage at Word and Phrase Levels
content_standard: B6.3.5.1: Apply the knowledge of verbs in communication
indicator_description: Use the imperative form of verb to give warning and express prohibitions
examplars: 1. E.g. Break the glass, and you will pay for it.

subject: English Language
strand: Grammar Usage at Word and Phrase Levels
content_standard: B6.3.6.1: Apply the knowledge of different types of adverbs in communication
indicator_description: Use adverbs to express degree and reason
examplars:
1. Revise adverbs of time and manner in context: Have learners listen to/ read several sentences containing adverbs of time and manner.
2. Use drills and games to provide practice in using adverbs of time. E.g. Game: Lucky Dip Drill: Substitution Table Drill
3. Learners sit in groups to discuss issues involving use of adverbs of time.
4. Repeat the procedure to introduce adverbs of degree and reason. e.g. Degree: The shirt is too small for me. You are making so much noise. Reason: I sing because I like singing.
5. Have groups construct short paragraphs using such adverbs.

subject: English Language
strand: Grammar Usage at Word and Phrase Levels
content_standard: B6.3.7.1: Understand and use idiomatic expressions appropriately in speech and in writing
indicator_description: identify, explain and use idiomatic expressions correctly.
examplars:
1. Revise idiomatic expressions in context to guide learners identify their meanings. E.g. i. Take after (to resemble). ii. To pull down(to destroy the reputation of someone).
2. Together with learners, identify and write some familiar/common idiomatic expressions from learners’ culture. Then introduce other idiomatic expressions from the native language. E.g. i. Take after (to resemble). ii. To pull down(to destroy the reputation of someone).
3. With examples from the dictionary, help learners write idioms and their explanations.
4. Provide a few idiomatic expressions and let learners interpret them.
5. Have groups of learners write idioms and their meanings on manila cards to hang in the classroom for a while.

subject: English Language
strand: Grammar Usage at Word and Phrase Levels
content_standard: B6.3.8.1: Apply the knowledge of conjunctions in speech and in writing
indicator_description: Identify and use simple conjunctions - so that, when, while, if etc. to: show purpose, express time, condition, etc.
examplars:
1. Revise the coordinating conjunctions – and, or, nor, but and subordinating conjunctions by having learners use them sentences.
2. Provide sentences for learners to join them with coordinating and subordinating conjunctions.
3. Provide passages and let learners identify conjunctions.
4. Let them do other activities like filling blanks with suitable conjunctions and using conjunction in their own sentences.
5. Introduce the conjunctions "so that, when, while, if etc" in context. e.g. Take a hot bath so that you can sleep well. I drank the soup while my mother was in the kitchen.
6. Provide opportunities for learners to use the conjunctions learnt.

subject: English Language
strand: Grammar Usage at Word and Phrase Levels
content_standard: B6.3.9.1: Apply the knowledge of modals in speech and in writing
indicator_description: Use modals to express a variety of meanings
examplars:
1. Revise modal auxiliaries. - Can: conveys ability - May: asks for permission, expresses politeness, possibility - Must: obligation or compulsion, necessity - Shall/will: prediction, intention, determination etc. - Could: tentativeness, politeness - Would: politeness etc. - Might: possibility - Should: obligation - Used to: for past activity/event - Have to/ought to/need to: for obligation
2. Introduce them in context one or two at a time.
3. With examples, assist learners to use the modals in sentences to convey specific meanings such as politeness.

subject: English Language
strand: Grammar Usage at Word and Phrase Levels
content_standard: B6.3.10.1: Apply the knowledge of prepositions in oral and written communication
indicator_description: Use prepositions to convey a variety of meanings: Time, Purpose, Possession, Comparison, Support or opposition
examplars:
1. Revise prepositions such as those that convey meanings of position, time, direction, possession and comparison. Write examples of sentences to illustrate this.
2. Have learners identify common prepositions (on, in, near, under) in sentences. Let them use these prepositions in sentences.
3. Introduce prepositions that show support or opposition in context. E.g. for you, against you. Let them identify these prepositions in texts they have read e.g. reading passages, stories, etc.
4. Learners now write their own sentences with the prepositions.

subject: English Language
strand: Grammar Usage at Word and Phrase Levels
content_standard: B6.3.11.1: show understanding of adjective phrases in oral and written communication.
indicator_description: Form and use adjective phrases correctly
examplars:
1. Revise adjectives in context by having learners identify them in sentences and use them in their own sentences.
2. Introduce the adjective phrase as a grammatical structure with the adjective as head. Note: The adjective phrase can be a single adjective or more. E.g. a beautiful house, a strong African oven.
3. Provide sentences with adjective phrases and let learners identify the adjective phrases.
4. In groups, learners form adjective phrases and use the phrases in sentences.

subject: English Language
strand: Grammar Usage at Word and Phrase Levels
content_standard: B6.3.12.1: show understanding of adverb phrases in oral and written communication.
indicator_description: Form and use adverb phrases correctly.
examplars:
1. Revise adverbs of manner, place and time.
2. Introduce the adverb phrase in context and get learners to understand that an adverb phrase is a grammatical structure that modifies a verb. It is usually a single word or more than a single word.
3. Provide several examples in context for learners to read out.
4. Provide short texts for learners to identify the adverb phrases.
5. Let learners use the adverb phrases in sentences. E.g. He comes to the house every day.

subject: English Language
strand: Grammar Usage at Word and Phrase Levels
content_standard: B6.3.13.1: show understanding of direct and reported speeches in oral and written communication.
indicator_description: Form and use reported speech appropriately
examplars:
1. Revision: Provide situations for learners to identify and use them in both present and past tenses.
2. Introduce the direct speech with several examples of sentences. E.g. “We saw an eagle,” said Aba. “Esi is tired,” said Mum. “Joe has become rich,” said Atongo.
3. Lead learners, with examples, to change direct speech into reported speech by: i. Introducing a reporting clause E.g. a. Aba said that …………… b. Mum said that………….. c. Atongo said that………. ii. Introducing “that” and completing the sentences. E.g. a. Aba said that they had seen an eagle. iii. Removing the quotation marks. E.g. b. Mum said that Esi was tired.
4. Put learners in pairs. Let one produce direct speech and let the other change it into reported speech. Let them change over after some time.

subject: English Language
content_standard: B6.4.2.1: Copy and rewrite sentences correctly
indicator_description: Write with a legible, fluent and personal handwriting style
examplars:
1. Have learners select sentences from their readers.
2. As learners observe, demonstrate the activity by writing the sentences in joint script on the board.
3. Have individual learners read out the sentences.
4. Let learners copy the sentences into their exercise books.
5. Have pupils share their work with their partners.

subject: English Language
content_standard: B6.4.2.1: Copy and rewrite sentences correctly
indicator_description: Write complex sentences clearly and correctly
examplars:
1. Revise simple and compound sentences.
2. Introduce the complex sentence structure by combining pairs of simple sentences. E.g. You will come. We will go. When you come, we will go.
3. Elicit several such sentences from learners.
4. Put learners into groups to identify complex sentences from texts they have read.
5. Have learners copy complex sentences into their exercise books.

subject: English Language
content_standard: B6.4.6.1: Develop, organise and express ideas cohesively in writing and representing for a variety of purposes, audience, and contexts
indicator_description: Choose appropriate ways and modes of writing for a variety of purposes, audience, and contexts, and organise facts, ideas and/or points of view in a way appropriate to the mode of delivery, using appropriate text features
examplars:
1. Work with learners to select sample texts from a variety of models of writing from learners’ readers and teacher’s resources e.g. newspapers, magazines etc. You may take learners to the library for this exercise. Examples of modes of writing. i. Process ii. Narrative iii. Descriptive iv. Persuasive v. Argumentative vi. Letter Note: Present one mode of writing at a time and at different times (in different lessons).
2. Put learners into small groups and assign each group a mode of writing.
3. Have learners study the sample text.
4. Guide them through appropriate questions to identify the: i. Mode of writing/types of essay ii. Purpose iii. Audience
5. Jumble the paragraphs and have learners re-arrange the paragraphs into a coherent piece.
6. Encourage learners to use search engines to search for the different modes of writing they have studied.

subject: English Language
content_standard: B6.4.6.1: Develop, organise and express ideas cohesively in writing and representing for a variety of purposes, audience, and contexts
indicator_description: use key words, phrases or clauses to introduce the main idea in the paragraph.
examplars:
1. Display a Model Paragraph.
2. Put learners into groups to read the paragraph and identify what the passage is about or the main idea.
3. Have them identify the sentence that contains the main idea.
4. Let the group identify the supporting ideas and what each sentence says about the main idea.
5. Ask each group choose a given topic and write five sentences about the topic.

subject: English Language
content_standard: B6.4.6.1: Develop, organise and express ideas cohesively in writing and representing for a variety of purposes, audience, and contexts
indicator_description: Elaborate on, explain and/or justify the main ideas of a paragraph by providing relevant details and examples
examplars:
1. Display a model paragraph.
2. By way of revision, have learners identify the main idea and supporting ideas.
3. Think-Pair-Share: Have pairs of learners discuss why a main idea in a paragraph needs elaboration, explanation or examples and share their views with peers and the whole class.
4. Give learners a topic sentence for them to elaborate on by providing details (explanation/examples). E.g. Topic sentence: Kofi is a good child.

subject: English Language
content_standard: B6.4.6.1: Develop, organise and express ideas cohesively in writing and representing for a variety of purposes, audience, and contexts
indicator_description: Use cohesive devices pronoun references, repeated key terms or grammatical structures to link ideas in a paragraph.
examplars:
1. Display a sample paragraph.
2. Underline the cohesive devices/transitional words.
3. Have learners read the paragraph and discuss with their peers what the underlined words are doing in the paragraph.
4. Lead them in a general discussion on what the underlined words are and their roles in the paragraph. E.g. First, second, third, finally, again, also, and, besides, further, in the first place, last, likewise, next, then, too.
5. Provide a paragraph and leave out the cohesive devices. Have learners complete the paragraph with appropriate transitional words.

subject: English Language
content_standard: B6.4.9.1: Apply the skills and strategies for idea generation, selection, development, organisation and revision in writing
indicator_description: Select a topic of choice on a national issue or from different learning areas, brainstorm ideas and organize them before writing
examplars:
1. Scaffold the writing process. i. Pre-writing
2. Have learners select and discuss a national issue with their partners.
3. Have learners brainstorm to generate as many ideas as possible about the topic.
4. Have them organise the ideas into a writing plan using an outline, a chart or an appropriate graphic organiser. E.g. line diagram

subject: English Language
content_standard: B6.4.9.2: Develop and express ideas coherently and cohesively in writing
indicator_description: Develop ideas into a three-paragraph draft without considering the writing conventions, linking ideas within and across paragraphs with a wider range of cohesive devices
examplars: 1. Put learners into groups to develop their ideas in B6.4.9.1.1 into a three-paragraph draft.

subject: English Language
content_standard: B6.4.9.3: Apply strategies for improving drafts for publishing
indicator_description: Review, and revise the draft to produce a coherent piece by proposing grammar and vocabulary for improvement.
examplars:
1. Have learners review their first draft by reading it slowly and critically several times to identify words/expressions that need to be cancelled.
2. Guide learners with the questions below: i. Are your ideas in order and easy to understand? ii. Are all the sentences talking about the main idea of the paragraph? iii. Are all the paragraphs talking about the topic?
3. Have learners read their partners’ draft and offer suggestions.
4. Encourage learners to make as many changes as are necessary to improve their drafts.
5. Have learners make a clean draft for publishing.

subject: English Language
content_standard: B6.4.9.3: Apply strategies for improving drafts for publishing
indicator_description: Edit/proofread draft, checking capitalisation, usage, punctuation and spelling
examplars:
1. Have learners pick up their clean draft (Refer to B5 4.3.1) and check for conventions. E.g. errors in capitalisation, punctuation and spelling. Tips for learners: i. Do my sentences have proper punctuation? E.g. full stop, question mark, exclamation mark. ii. Have I used commas correctly? iii. Do my sentences start with capital letters? iv. Have I capitalised proper nouns?
2. Have learners do peer editing.
3. Have them prepare neat final copies.
4. Guide them to proofread the final copies before publishing.

subject: English Language
content_standard: B6.4.9.3: Apply strategies for improving drafts for publishing
indicator_description: Publish writing piece for other peers to read and in the school magazine.
examplars:
1. Revise writing as a process.
2. Have learners share their writing with their peers classmates and families.
3. Encourage learners to type/copy neatly their writing and submit them for publication in the class magazine, school magazine/notice board. Other places to publish include The Junior Graphic or The Daily Graphic and other print media.

subject: English Language
content_standard: B6.4.10.1: Narrate situations, express feelings and convey point of view about the world/ or fictional world
indicator_description: Edit/proofread draft, checking capitalisation, usage, punctuation and spelling
examplars:
1. Reflective Writing A reflective writing explores who you are now and who you were before now. It focuses on a change you have experienced and reflects on how that change makes you unique.
2. Take learners through the writing process. Then Now chart Then Now Reasons for change
3. Use questions to revise: Prewriting – strategy Make a list of how things used to be and how they are now. Tell why they changed. Continue with the rest of the process: Writing, Revising, Editing and Publishing.

subject: English Language
content_standard: B6.4.11.1: Create texts
indicator_description: Write freely about topics of choice on national issues and issues from different learning areas.
examplars:
1. Have learners select a topic of their choice on national issues and issues from different learning areas.
2. Guide learners to brainstorm and generate ideas.
3. Have learners organise their ideas to write their first draft.
4. They revise their first draft.
5. Learners then, peer edit their work.
6. Have them present their work for class discussion and correction.
7. They then write the final draft and display their work for their peers to read.

subject: English Language
content_standard: B6.4.11.1: Create texts
indicator_description: Write poems and imaginative narrative stories using ideas from poems and imaginative texts read or viewed
examplars:
1. Select a narrative text or story to revise the elements of narrative: - Title - Characters - Setting - Plot/storyline - Conflict, dialogue
2. Note: Through discussion and reference to familiar stories, show that: - Stories have people (characters) who face a problem (conflict) in a particular place and time (setting). They also show what people do (action) and say (dialogue) to resolve the problem.
3. Discuss a short text to guide learners to go through the writing process to write their stories.
4. Put pupils into small groups to plan their writing. - Create characters - Create conflict - Establish a setting - Plan action and dialogue
5. They write the first draft, revise, edit and publish their writing by following the stages of the writing process.
6. Poems and Plays: Discuss sentences of poems and plays. Guide learners through the writing process to write poems and plays.

subject: English Language
content_standard: B6.4.12.1: Demonstrate knowledge of descriptive words/expressions in writing
indicator_description: Use descriptive words/sound devices/ figurative language to describe events/situations/places/personal experiences and events.
examplars:
1. Briefly revise the writing process by having learners name the stages and say what happens at each stage.
2. Revise descriptive writing with learners
3. Have learners select a topic e.g. “A Day I will never forget”.
4. Provide a sample text.
5. Guide learners to identify the descriptive words and expressions:
6. Discuss the descriptive words and expressions with learners.
7. Put learners into groups and guide them through the writing process to describe events/situations/places of their choice and personal experiences.
8. Focus on the use of: - Descriptive (adjectives) words. - Figurative language e.g. simile – metaphor, personification, sound devices
9. Note: Sensory details are experiences through the senses: sight, smell, touch, taste. - Sensory details (Allow a reader to visualize a person, a place, a thing or an idea).

subject: English Language
content_standard: B6.4.13.1: Support an opinion in writing
indicator_description: Write explanations and examples to support opinions
examplars:
1. Present a motion to learners. E. g. A lawyer is more important than a doctor.
2. Discuss the meaning and implication of the motion with pupils and invite them to take a stand either for or against the motion.
3. Put learners into two groups: for the motion and against the motion and write down what they think about it.
4. Groups read out their opinions to the class for discussions.

subject: English Language
content_standard: B6.4.13.2: Write arguments to support claims with clear reasons and relevant evidence
indicator_description: Introduce claims and support with clear reasons in order of importance and evidence using credible sources and demonstrate understanding of the topic or text.
examplars:
1. Working in groups, learners provide reasons for the opinions they hold on a topic.
2. Guide groups to provide evidence for the opinions they hold.
3. Have groups present their work for discussions.

subject: English Language
content_standard: B6.4.13.2: Write arguments to support claims with clear reasons and relevant evidence
indicator_description: Use words, phrases, and clauses to clarify the relationships among claim(s) and reasons.
examplars:
1. Lead learners to discuss appropriate language for arguments e. g. use of adjectives in their various forms.
2. You may read out model argumentative essays to guide learners.

subject: English Language
content_standard: B6.4.13.2: Write arguments to support claims with clear reasons and relevant evidence
indicator_description: Establish and maintain a formal style.
examplars:
1. Using models, discuss the basic structure of an argumentative piece: - Introduction. - Reasons for the stand taken. - Conclusion.
2. Have learners in groups to present full compositions using class discussions.

subject: English Language
content_standard: B6.4.13.2: Write arguments to support claims with clear reasons and relevant evidence
indicator_description: Provide a concluding statement that follows from argument presented and pose a rhetorical question on the topic.
examplars:
1. Have them go through the writing process to present/state an opinion, explain and justify it so as to persuade the reader to accept the opinion or point of view.
2. Teach the features of a debate e.g. vocatives, taking a stand etc. Put learners in groups and have them select a controversial or debatable topic. Divide the class into two to prepare using the writing process as a guide, and to debate on the topic.

subject: English Language
content_standard: B6.4.14.2: Write the event of the day
indicator_description: Write short paragraphs to describe incidents. E.g. accidents, fire outbreak.
examplars:
1. Briefly revise the writing process by having learners recall the stages and explain the features and relevance of each stage.
2. Have learners select a topic and brainstorm to generate ideas.
3. Put them into groups to organise the points for the development of paragraphs.
4. Assign learners to individually develop the points into outlines and then into a draft.
5. Let them do self and peer-editing before finally presenting the final work.
6. Learners present their finished work in groups.

subject: English Language
content_standard: B6.4.14.2: Write the event of the day
indicator_description: Write articles on varied topics

subject: English Language
content_standard: B6.4.15.1: Write informal letters on given topics
indicator_description: Write to friends to express their views on given topics using appropriate letter formats
examplars:
1. Discuss the features of informal letters with learners.
2. Give examples of formal letters to them and let them talk about the similarities and differences between formal and informal letters.
3. Brainstorm to guide learners generate topics they would want to write on and let each group choose a topic.
4. Learners identify purpose and audience and make a mind map to guide the writing.
5. Each group writes an informal letter and edits it. The letters are passed round the groups for editing.

subject: English Language
content_standard: B6.5.1.1: Use capital letters to start the first word in direct speech.
indicator_description: Follow appropriate mechanical convention.
examplars: 1. Ask individual learners to write a formal letter each on a given topic, paying attention to correct use of capital letters, the full stop, comma, question mark, exclamation mark, quotation marks, apostrophe, hyphen etc.

subject: English Language
content_standard: B6.5.2.1: Show understanding of how punctuations are used appropriately in writing
indicator_description: Use : the comma around a phrase for the modifying of the proceeding noun; quotation marks to indicate direct speech; apostrophe to show plural possession;
examplars:
1. Revise punctuations with learners. - Give out samples of relevant texts to groups of learners to identify the punctuation marks used. e.g. 1. Kofi, my friend, will help me do my homework 2. I like football," said Ama. 3. This is girls' work. 4. Mary: Kofi, do you like my pen? Kofi: Yes, I do.
2. Write a text on the board, leaving out the punctuation marks.
3. Have the whole class punctuate it.
4. Have them study unpunctuated texts individually for a few minutes and then re-write the texts using appropriate punctuation marks where they have been omitted.

subject: English Language
content_standard: B6.5.3.1: Apply knowledge of different types of nouns in communication
indicator_description: Identify and use nouns or noun phrases to describe conditions.

subject: English Language
content_standard: B6.5.3.1: Apply knowledge of different types of nouns in communication
indicator_description: Identify and use: proper nouns to refer to organisations/events, Count/non-count, Singular, Plural (regular, irregular), without plural marker, Gender
examplars:
1. Revise all the types of nouns (refer to B1-B5).
2. Provide learners with opportunities to further practice using these nouns. E.g. a. Learners take their reading books and identify and make a list of these nouns, use them in sentences and present their work to the class for feedback. b. Use language drills and games to help learners to practice. e.g. a pair drill for singular/plural nouns - Learner “A” gives a noun – mango - Learner “B” gives its plural form “mangoes” and uses it in a sentence – I love mangoes.
3. Provide further practice activities to build on learners' knowledge on nouns.
4. Proper nouns: Introduce the concept in context. e.g. Christmas is coming. The people of Ga celebrate Homowo.
5. Count and non-count nouns: Revise the concept of proper nouns and common nouns giving and eliciting several examples. (Proper nouns refer to particular nouns such as names of people, places, mountains, etc.) Provide a text and let learners identify count and non-count nouns. Have groups use the nouns identified in simple sentences. Invite groups to present their work.
6. Singular and Plural (regular, irregular) nouns: Introduce singular and plural nouns in context. E.g. I need a table. Can you give me one of these tables? This child looks sick, but those children look healthy. Have learners read the sentences and observe the underlined words. Show pictures of objects/people to help learners identify regular and irregular nouns. E. g. picture of a child and a picture of children. Guide learners to discover the difference between regular and irregular plural forms: - Regular count nouns take s or es to form their plural. E.g. table – tables, chair – chairs - Irregular count nouns are nouns that do not take s or es to form their plural. E.g. child – children; ox – oxen; man – men; goose – geese. Put learners into groups. Have groups construct sentences with given singular and plural nouns and share their work with the class.
7. Plural nouns without plural markers: Have learners discover the plural nouns without plural markers in context.

subject: English Language
content_standard: B6.5.3.1: Apply knowledge of different types of nouns in communication
indicator_description: Use the "-ing" nouns to refer to activities.
examplars: 1. e.g. I enjoy singing. I like swimming.

subject: English Language
content_standard: B6.5.3.1: Apply knowledge of different types of nouns in communication
indicator_description: Identify and use abstract nouns to refer to concepts and ideas.
examplars:
1. Revise abstract nouns.
2. Have learners identify abstract nouns in texts and use them in sentences e.g. intelligence, knowledge, idea, patience i. I admire Ama because of her patience. ii. He appears to have a good knowledge of what we are saying.

subject: English Language
content_standard: B6.5.4.1: Demonstrate understanding of verbs in everyday language
indicator_description: Differentiate between how the simple past and the present perfect tense forms are used in speech and in writing
examplars:
1. Revise the simple present and present perfect tenses by using examples and situations.
2. Distribute copies of a sample story and let them identify the simple past verbs, how they are used in sentences and identify modals used. Use this as a guide to let learners write a story using the simple past.
3. Learners in pairs write their own stories making sure they use both tense forms.
4. Prepare a grid containing all that could be needed to guide the pairs to do their own editing paying attention to the correct use both tense.

subject: English Language
content_standard: B6.5.4.1: Demonstrate understanding of verbs in everyday language
indicator_description: Explore the use of the simple past verb form and participle form
examplars:
1. Let learners write a story. E.g. about what happened after school on Monday.
2. Discuss the essay topic with learners and use the webbing strategy to help them write on the chosen topic. Learners write individually.
3. Learners exchange their work for editing as teacher guides by focusing on the past verb form and participle form.
4. Assist those whose essays are only in the simple past to some in the past participle tense.
5. Learners rewrite their essays incorporating the corrections.
6. Have learners write on another topic individually using the simple past verb form as well as the past participle form.

subject: English Language
content_standard: B6.5.4.1: Demonstrate understanding of verbs in everyday language
indicator_description: Use a range of verb forms in speech and in writing.
examplars:
1. Guide learners, with examples, to form sentences in the past perfect tense. Learners narrate a short event using the simple past and past perfect tenses.
2. Guide learners with examples to construct sentences using the past perfect form of verbs. E.g. When you came I had already finished the work.
3. Let learners narrate short events using both tenses.
4. Let learners write a letter to an elderly relative on something that happened in the past.
5. Learners in their groups (mixed ability), choose a topic or event they will want to write on using the past perfect and simple past tenses.
6. Have learners write a mind map to guide their writing. Learners edit their writing at every stage e.g. introduction, body and conclusion. Encourage them to share their work with other groups.

subject: English Language
content_standard: B6.5.4.1: Demonstrate understanding of verbs in everyday language
indicator_description: Apply the use of the singular and plural subject and the verb forms that go with them.
examplars:
1. Learners write on a topic using the simple present, present perfect and past perfect tenses.
2. Discuss the topic with learners contributing with sentences containing the present perfect and past perfect.
3. Let learners write in bits e.g. First start with introduction and guide them to edit.
4. Learners then write a whole essay on a chosen topic individually

subject: English Language
content_standard: B6.5.5.1: Demonstrate understanding of adjectives in speech and in writing
indicator_description: Use superlative forms of regular and irregular adjectives to make comparison
examplars:
1. Revise adjectives by having learners describe various classroom objects and people.
2. Provide samples of texts demonstrating the use of the comparative and superlative forms of regular/irregular adjectives.
